title: Rewrite the Quadlet documentation.
body:
This commit does the following:

- Splits the podman-systemd.unit.5.md into multiple files - one for each quadlet file type, podman-quadlet.7.md for general quadlet information and podman-quadlet-basic-usage.7.md for quadlet examples.
- Removes the original podman-systemd.unit.5.md file.
- Adds support for jinja2 templating language in the markdown_preprocess.
- Uses new logic in markdown_preprocess in options/*.md to use the single .md file for both podman subcommands man-pages and quadlet man-pages. This deduplicates the Quadlet man-pages a lot.
- Adds new `@@option quadlet:source.md` preprocess command to import such .md files from options directory.


#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

```release-note
None
```

comments.body: [NON-BLOCKING] Packit jobs failed. @containers/packit-build please check. Everyone else, feel free to ignore.
did a quick pass like @Luap99 ... looks like you need to repush at least to fix validate issues.  @ygalblum do you think you could also spend a little time reviewing this large PR?
Still working on the `hack/xref-quadlet-docs` script and issues found by it.

@ygalblum , I used templates where they existed and kept the version without template if the template did not exist. Thinking about it now, I probably could add templates to options/ directory for everything really.

> I used templates where they existed and kept the version without template if the template did not exist

I figured this was the case. As I wrote, the problem is that now the description is inconsistent. Some keys are described with what they do while other kept the old form of `equivalent to ...`
